expressways.name: NLEX
expressways.coverage: Metro Manila to Pampanga — approx. 84 km
expressways.patrolNote: NLEX Patrol units operate 24/7 along the full expressway.
expressways.kmMarkerNote: Green kilometer markers (KM posts) are visible every 500 meters. Note your KM reading before calling.
instructions: Pull over safely to the right shoulder. Turn on hazard lights immediately.
Note your kilometer marker — it is displayed on green posts along the road.
Call the NLEX emergency line. Provide your kilometer marker, direction (northbound or southbound), and vehicle plate number.
Stay inside your vehicle with your seatbelt on if it is safe to do so.
Wait for NLEX Patrol — do not walk along the expressway.
If there are injuries, call 911 in addition to the NLEX emergency line.
doNot: Do not stop in active traffic lanes.
Do not walk along the expressway.
Do not attempt repairs in a traffic lane.
Do not make U-turns on the expressway.

expressways.name: SLEX
expressways.coverage: Metro Manila to Laguna and Quezon Province
expressways.patrolNote: SLEX Road Rangers operate around the clock for breakdowns and accidents.
expressways.kmMarkerNote: Kilometer markers are posted along the route. Note your kilometer reading and direction when calling.
instructions: Pull to the right emergency lane immediately. Turn on hazard lights.
Note your kilometer marker and your direction (northbound or southbound).
Call the SLEX emergency line. Provide your plate number, vehicle type, and exact location.
If there are injuries, call 911.
Do not attempt to push or move the vehicle unless directed by road rangers.
Wait for assistance. Do not flag down passing vehicles on the expressway.
doNot: Do not stop in live traffic lanes.
Do not walk or stand on the road.
Do not self-rescue in dangerous conditions.
Do not make U-turns.

expressways.name: Skyway
expressways.patrolNote: Skyway patrol units respond to incidents on the elevated road sections.
expressways.kmMarkerNote: Location markers are posted by segment (Stage 1, 2, or 3). Note the segment and kilometer reading when calling.
instructions: Do not stop in traffic — continue to the nearest designated emergency bay or expressway exit.
Turn on hazard lights.
Call the Skyway emergency line immediately.
Provide your location: segment (Stage 1/2/3), kilometer marker, and direction.
If injuries are present, call 911.
Skyway is an elevated road — do not exit the vehicle near the edge or guardrails.
doNot: Do not stop outside designated emergency bays.
Do not walk to the edge or past guardrails.
Do not attempt to push or tow on the elevated section.
Do not reverse on the expressway.

expressways.name: SCTEX
expressways.coverage: Tarlac to Subic Bay — approx. 94 km
expressways.patrolNote: SCTEX patrol responds to incidents along the full route, though response times may be longer in remote sections.
expressways.kmMarkerNote: Green kilometer markers are posted every 500 meters. Essential for accurate location reporting on this long route.
instructions: Pull to the right shoulder. Activate hazard lights.
Note your kilometer marker — this is essential on this long, less-trafficked route.
Call the SCTEX emergency number. State your kilometer marker, direction, and vehicle details.
Call 911 for any injuries.
Stay in the vehicle with doors locked while waiting for patrol.
SCTEX spans a long distance — wait time for patrol may be longer than urban expressways.
doNot: Do not walk along the highway.
Do not stop in the middle of the road.
Do not hitchhike — wait for official patrol.
Do not leave the vehicle unattended in an unsafe location.

expressways.name: TPLEX
expressways.coverage: Tarlac to San Juan, La Union — approx. 89 km
expressways.patrolNote: TPLEX patrol units are deployed along the full expressway length.
expressways.kmMarkerNote: Kilometer markers are posted at regular intervals. Always note your KM reading before calling.
instructions: Move to the right shoulder immediately. Turn on hazard lights.
Note your kilometer marker and direction (northbound or southbound).
Call TPLEX emergency. Provide your kilometer marker, vehicle plate, and number of passengers.
Call 911 if there are injuries.
Remain in your vehicle while waiting for patrol assistance.
doNot: Do not stop in traffic lanes.
Do not walk on the expressway.
Do not attempt to flag down passing vehicles.
Do not make U-turns.
